glosses.en: wife / your wife / his wife / married lady / madam

sentences.target: こちらが私の奥さんです。
sentences.reading: こちらがわたくしのおくさんです。

tokens.explanation: Used by the speaker to introduce or refer to someone close to them.
Marks the subject of the sentence.
Refers to the speaker themself.
Indicates possession or a genitive relationship.
Polite term meaning ‘wife’, referring to the speaker’s spouse.
Polite copula used to end the sentence formally.
Period marking the end of the sentence.

sentences.target: 隣の奥さんはいつも笑顔ですね。
sentences.reading: となりのおくさんはいつもえがおですね。

translations.en: The neighbor's wife is always smiling, isn't she?
translations.es: La esposa del vecino siempre está sonriendo, ¿no?
translations.fr: La femme du voisin est toujours souriante, n’est-ce pas ?
translations.de: Die Nachbarsfrau lächelt immer, nicht wahr?
translations.it: La moglie del vicino sorride sempre, non è vero?
translations.zh-Hans: 邻居的老婆总是面带微笑，不是吗？
translations.yue: 鄰居嘅老婆成日面帶微笑，唔系吗？
translations.ru: Жена соседа всегда улыбается, не правда ли?
translations.pt: A esposa do vizinho está sempre sorrindo, não é?
translations.tr: Komşunun karısı her zaman gülümsüyor, değil mi?
translations.vi: Vợ của người hàng xóm luôn mỉm cười, phải không?
translations.ar: زوجة الجيران تبتسم دائما ، أليس كذلك؟
translations.nl: De vrouw van de buren lacht altijd, nietwaar?
translations.uk: Сусідська дружина завжди посміхається, чи не так?
translations.hu: A szomszéd felesége mindig mosolyog, nem?
translations.hi: पड़ोसी की पत्नी हमेशा मुस्कुराती रहती है, है ना?
translations.pl: Żona sąsiada zawsze się uśmiecha, prawda?
translations.el: Η γυναίκα του γείτονα είναι πάντα χαμογελαστή, έτσι δεν είναι;
translations.nb: Naboens kone smiler alltid, ikke sant?
translations.sv: Grannens fru ler alltid, eller hur?
translations.ro: Soția vecinului zâmbește mereu, nu-i așa?
translations.cs: Sousedova žena se pořád usmívá, že?
translations.da: Naboens kone smiler altid, ikke sandt?
translations.fi: Naapurin vaimo hymyilee aina, eikö niin?

tokens.explanation: Refers to the neighboring house or person next door.
Links ‘next door’ to ‘wife’ in a possessive sense.
Refers to the neighbor’s wife.
Marks ‘neighbor’s wife’ as the topic of the sentence.
Indicates that the action happens all the time.
Describes the facial expression of smiling.
Polite copula linking the topic and its description.
Sentence-ending particle seeking agreement or confirmation.
Period marking the end of the sentence.

sentences.target: 奥さん、そちらに忘れ物がありますよ。
sentences.reading: おくさん、そちらにわすれものがありますよ。

translations.en: Madam, you've left something behind over there.
translations.fr: Madame, vous avez oublié quelque chose là-bas.
translations.de: Madame, Sie haben da drüben etwas zurückgelassen.
translations.it: Signora, lei ha lasciato qualcosa laggiù.
translations.zh-Hans: 女士，你在那儿留下了什么东西。
translations.yue: 女士，你喺度留低咗乜嘢嘢。
translations.pt: Senhora, você deixou algo para trás lá.
translations.vi: Thưa bà, bà đã để lại thứ gì đó ở đằng kia.
translations.ar: سيدتي ، لقد تركت شيئا وراءك هناك.
translations.nl: Mevrouw, u heeft daar iets achtergelaten.
translations.hi: मैडम, आपने वहां पर कुछ पीछे छोड़ दिया है।
translations.el: Κυρία, έχετε αφήσει κάτι πίσω εκεί.
translations.nb: Frue, du har lagt igjen noe der borte.
translations.id: Nyonya, Anda telah meninggalkan sesuatu di sana.
translations.sv: Frun, ni har lämnat något kvar där borta.
translations.ro: Doamnă, ați lăsat ceva în urmă acolo.
translations.da: Frue, du har efterladt noget derovre.

tokens.explanation: Vocative use addressing a woman politely (equivalent to ‘ma’am’).
Comma separating the vocative from the rest of the sentence.
Refers to the location near the listener.
Marks the location where something exists.
Refers to something that has been left behind.
Marks ‘forgotten item’ as the subject of existence.
Existential verb indicating that something exists.
Sentence-ending particle adding emphasis or informing the listener.
Period marking the end of the sentence.

sentences.target: 彼の奥さんは料理が上手です。
sentences.reading: かれのおくさんはりょうりがじょうずです。

translations.en: His wife is good at cooking.
translations.fr: Sa femme est douée pour la cuisine.
translations.it: Sua moglie è brava a cucinare.
translations.pt: Sua esposa é boa em cozinhar.
translations.vi: Vợ anh ấy nấu ăn giỏi.
translations.hi: उसकी पत्नी खाना बनाने में अच्छी है।
translations.pl: Jego żona jest dobra w gotowaniu.
translations.el: Η γυναίκα του είναι καλή στο μαγείρεμα.
translations.nb: Kona hans er flink til å lage mat.
translations.sv: Hans fru är bra på att laga mat.
translations.ro: Soția lui este bună la gătit.
translations.cs: Jeho žena je dobrá ve vaření.
translations.da: Hans kone er god til at lave mad.

tokens.explanation: Third-person pronoun referring to a male person.
Genitive particle linking ‘he’ and ‘wife’.
Refers to his wife.
Marks ‘his wife’ as the topic of the sentence.
Refers to the activity or art of preparing food.
Marks ‘cooking’ as the subject for ‘skilled’.
Na-adjective meaning ‘good at’ or ‘skillful’.
Polite copula to end the sentence.
Period marking the end of the sentence.

en: The neighbor's wife is always smiling, isn't she?
Madam, you've left something behind over there.
His wife is good at cooking.
jp: こちらが私の奥さんです。
隣の奥さんはいつも笑顔ですね。
奥さん、そちらに忘れ物がありますよ。
彼の奥さんは料理が上手です。
fu: こちらがわたしのおくさんです。
となりのおくさんはいつもえがおですね。
おくさん、そちらにわすれものがありますよ。
かれのおくさんはりょうりがじょうずです。
